description: Full responsive design overhaul — hamburger nav, responsive layouts for all views, mobile-friendly modals and tables

userStories.title: Global responsive breakpoints and base styles
userStories.description: As a developer, I need consistent breakpoint variables and base responsive styles so all views use the same responsive behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CSS custom properties in base.css or main.css for breakpoints: --bp-phone: 768px, --bp-tablet: 1024px
Add global box-sizing: border-box rule if not already present
Confirm viewport meta tag in index.html: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
Add .table-scroll-container utility class: overflow-x auto, width 100%
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create AppNavbar component — desktop layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a consistent navigation bar component so all pages share the same nav.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/components/AppNavbar.vue
Desktop layout (>=1024px): horizontal bar with app name/logo on left, nav links (Dashboard, Profile, Admin if staff) in center/right, logout button on far right
Active route link is visually highlighted
Uses auth store for user info, isStaff, logout action
Uses Vue Router for navigation and active route detection
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 1280px width

userStories.title: AppNavbar — hamburger menu for mobile/tablet
userStories.description: As a user, I want the navbar to collapse into a hamburger menu on small screens so I can navigate on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: Below 1024px: nav links hidden, hamburger icon button shown
Clicking hamburger toggles a dropdown/overlay menu with all nav links
Menu closes when a link is clicked
Menu closes when clicking outside the menu
Hamburger icon animates to X when open (CSS transition)
Minimum 44px touch target for hamburger button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px width

userStories.title: Integrate AppNavbar into all views
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to replace per-view navbar code with the shared AppNavbar component.
acceptanceCriteria: Import and use AppNavbar in DashboardView, GroupDetailView, ProfileView, AdminView
Remove existing inline navbar markup from each view
LoginView does NOT use AppNavbar (no nav needed on login page)
Each view still renders its own content below the navbar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px and 1280px widths

userStories.title: Responsive Login View
userStories.description: As a user, I want the login page to look correct on all screen sizes and orientations.
acceptanceCriteria: Login card centers vertically and horizontally on all screen sizes
Card does not overflow viewport in phone landscape (short viewport height) — use max-height with overflow-y auto if needed
Form inputs and button have min 44px height for touch targets
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px x 667px and 667px x 375px

userStories.title: Responsive Dashboard View
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard group cards to reflow properly on all screen sizes.
acceptanceCriteria: Group card grid: 1 column on phone (<768px), 2 columns on tablet (768-1023px), 3+ on desktop (>=1024px)
Cards don't overflow or get clipped in landscape orientation
Balance summary section stacks vertically on screens below 768px
No horizontal body overflow at any width
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px, 768px, and 1280px widths

userStories.title: Responsive Group Detail View — expense list and actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want the group detail expense list and action buttons to be usable on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Expense list items stack content vertically on screens below 768px (no horizontal overflow)
Action buttons (add expense, settle) remain visible and accessible — don't overlap content
Action buttons stack vertically or shrink gracefully on phone widths
Tab/section navigation is horizontally scrollable if it overflows on small screens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px and 768px widths

userStories.title: Responsive Group Detail View — balances and debts
userStories.description: As a user, I want balance and debt summaries to reflow on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Balance summary cards reflow to single column on phone (<768px)
Debt/settlement lists stack vertically on narrow screens
No horizontal overflow on any group detail section
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px width

userStories.title: Responsive modal forms
userStories.description: As a user, I want modal forms to be usable on small screens and in landscape orientation.
acceptanceCriteria: Modals take full width (minus 16px margin) on screens below 480px
Modal content scrolls vertically when it exceeds viewport height
Form inputs have min 44px height for touch-friendly sizing
Submit/cancel buttons remain visible at bottom of modal (sticky or visible after scroll)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px x 667px and 667px x 375px

userStories.title: Responsive Profile View
userStories.description: As a user, I want the profile page to work on all screen sizes.
acceptanceCriteria: Avatar upload area and form fields stack vertically on phone (<768px)
API key section text wraps or truncates with ellipsis on narrow screens (no horizontal overflow)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px and 768px widths

userStories.title: Responsive Admin View
userStories.description: As an admin, I want admin data tables to be accessible on small screens via horizontal scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap all admin tables in .table-scroll-container for horizontal scroll on small screens
Table headers remain readable (no extreme column compression)
Admin add/edit forms adapt to narrow screens (inputs stack vertically below 768px)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill at 375px and 768px widths
